revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sato Makoto]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''佐藤 誠実'''（さとう せいじつ、[[1839年]][[12月28日]]（[[天保]]10年[[11月13日 (旧暦)|11月13日]]）- [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）[[3月31日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[国学|国学者]]である<ref name=":0">デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BD%90%E8%97%A4%E8%AA%A0%E5%AE%9F-18031 佐藤誠実]』- [[コトバンク]]</ref><ref name=":1">朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞出版]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BD%90%E8%97%A4%E8%AA%A0%E5%AE%9F-18031 佐藤誠実]』- コトバンク</ref>。幼名は造酒、号は黙斎<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />。
== 経歴・人物 == [[江戸]]の[[浅草]]（現在の[[東京都]][[台東区]]）[[正行寺 (台東区)|正行寺]]にて生まれる<ref name=":1" />。幼年期より同寺院の[[住職]]となった<ref name=":2">20世紀日本人名事典（[[日外アソシエーツ]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BD%90%E8%97%A4%20%E8%AA%A0%E5%AE%9F-1646059 佐藤 誠実]』- コトバンク</ref>。後に和漢の学問について学び初め<ref name=":1" />、[[黒川春村|村]]から国学や[[安積艮斎]]か[[儒教|儒学]]を学んだ<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />。
[[明治維新]]により[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[文部省]]（現在の[[文部科学省]]）に入省し<ref name=":0" />、後に[[帝国議会]]の前身である[[元老院 (日本)|元老院]]で勤め始めた<ref name=":2" />。[[1880年]]（明治13年）には[[廣池千九郎]]らと共に『[[古事類苑]]』全355冊の編纂に携わり<ref name=":1" />、後に編纂長となり元老院生に編纂の指導に携わった<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" />。後にこの[[百科事典]]は、[[1907年]]（明治41年）に完成させている<ref name=":2" />。この業績により、[[1899年]]（明治32年）には[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]に認定された<ref name=":2" />。同時期に[[東京音楽学校 (旧制)|東京音楽学校]]や東京帝国大学（現在の[[東京大学]]）古典講習科で教鞭を執った<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />。
== 著書 ==
* 『日本教育史』<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />- 支藩学校の教科書により作成された教科書。2巻からなる<ref name=":2" />。 * 『語学指南』<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}{{Academic-bio-stub}} [[Category:国学者]] [[Category:幕末の学者]] [[Category:文学博士取得者]] [[Category:帝国学士院会員]] [[Category:東京大学の教員]] [[Category:東京芸術大学の教員 (音楽)]] [[Category:武蔵国の人物]] [[Category:東京都区部出身の人物]] [[Category:1839年生]] [[Category:1908年没]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さとう しようしつ}}

[[明治維新]]により[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[文部省]]（現在の[[文部科学省]]）に入省し<ref name=":0" />、後に[[帝国議会]]の前身である[[元老院 (日本)|元老院]]で勤め始めた<ref name=":2" />。[[1880年]]（明治13年）には[[廣池千九郎]]らと共に『[[古事類苑]]』全355冊の編纂に携わり<ref name=":1" />、後に編纂長となり元老院生に編纂の指導に携わった<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" />。後にこの[[百科事典]]は、[[1907年]]（明治41年）に完成させている<ref name=":2" />。この業績により、[[1899年]]（明治32年）には[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]に認定された<ref name=":2" />。同時期に[[東京音楽学校 (旧制)|東京音楽学校]]や東京帝国大学（現在の[[東京大学]]）古典講習科で教鞭を執った<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />。

revision.text:
'''佐藤 誠実'''（さとう じょうじつ/のぶざね、[[1839年]][[12月28日]]（[[天保]]10年[[11月13日 (旧暦)|11月13日]]）- [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）[[3月31日]]）は、[[幕末]]から明治時代の[[国学|国学者]]である<ref name=":0">デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus（[[講談社]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BD%90%E8%97%A4%E8%AA%A0%E5%AE%9F-18031 佐藤誠実]』- [[コトバンク]]</ref><ref name=":1">朝日日本歴史人物事典（[[朝日新聞出版]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BD%90%E8%97%A4%E8%AA%A0%E5%AE%9F-18031 佐藤誠実]』- コトバンク</ref>。幼名は造酒、号は黙斎<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />。
== 経歴・人物 == [[江戸]]の[[浅草]]（現在の[[東京都]][[台東区]]）[[正行寺 (台東区)|正行寺]]にて生まれる<ref name=":1" />。幼年期より同寺院の[[住職]]となった<ref name=":2">20世紀日本人名事典（[[日外アソシエーツ]]）『[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BD%90%E8%97%A4%20%E8%AA%A0%E5%AE%9F-1646059 佐藤 誠実]』- コトバンク</ref>。後に和漢の学問について学び初め<ref name=":1" />、[[黒川春村]]から国学や[[安積艮斎]]から[[儒教|儒学]]を学んだ<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />。
[[明治維新]]により[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[文部省]]（現在の[[文部科学省]]）に入省し<ref name=":0" />、後に[[帝国議会]]の前身である[[元老院 (日本)|元老院]]で勤め始めた<ref name=":2" />。[[1880年]]（明治13年）には[[廣池千九郎]]らと共に『[[古事類苑]]』全355冊の編纂に携わった<ref name=":1" />。後に編纂長となり<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />、元老院生に編纂の指導に携わった<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" />。後にこの[[百科事典]]は、[[1907年]]（明治41年）に完成させている<ref name=":2" />。この業績により、[[1899年]]（明治32年）には[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]に認定された<ref name=":2" />。同時期に[[東京音楽学校 (旧制)|東京音楽学校]]や東京帝国大学（現在の[[東京大学]]）古典講習科で教鞭を執った<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />。
== 著書 ==
* 『日本教育史』<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />- [[師範学校]]の[[教科書]]として作成された<ref name=":2" />。全2巻からなる<ref name=":2" />。 * 『語学指南』<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{Normdaten}} {{Academic-bio-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さとう しようしつ}} [[Category:19世紀の国学者]] [[Category:幕末の学者]] [[Category:文学博士取得者]] [[Category:帝国学士院会員]] [[Category:戦前日本の文部官僚]] [[Category:日本の百科事典編集者]] [[Category:東京大学の教員]] [[Category:東京芸術大学の教員 (音楽)]] [[Category:武蔵国の人物]] [[Category:東京都区部出身の人物]] [[Category:1839年生]] [[Category:1908年没]]

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=July 2023}}
'''Yūsoku Kojitsu''' (有職故実) refers to the study and traditions of ancient customs, regulations, systems, etiquette, ceremonies, attire, and practices of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|朝廷 (日本)}} and {{ill|samurai class|ja|武家}}. The term also encompasses the study of these traditions.
== Overview ==
"Yūsoku" denotes knowledge of past precedents, while "kojitsu" refers to persuasive grounds or norms regarding public and private conduct. Individuals well-versed in these areas were referred to as '''yūsokusha''' (有職者), a term later shortened to "yūsoku."
== Pre-Edo Period ==
=== Court Traditions ===
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the systematization of precedents advanced under the regency of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadahira|ja|藤原忠平}}, who established the basic forms of court rituals. These rituals were rationalized using the knowledge of Tadahira’s father, {{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|ja|藤原基経}}, and his elder brother, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|ja|藤原時平}}, along with imperial orders and references to {{ill|Geki diaries|ja|外記日記}}. This tradition was orally transmitted to his sons, forming two major schools of court ritual: the {{ill|Ono no Miya school|ja|小野宮流}} of his elder son, {{ill|Fujiwara no Saneyori|ja|藤原実頼}}, and the {{ill|Kujo school|ja|九条流}} of his younger son, {{ill|Fujiwara no Morosuke|ja|藤原師輔}}.<ref>{{ill|Otsu Toru|ja|大津透}}, ''Michinaga and the Court Society: Japanese History 06'', Kodansha Academic Library, ISBN 978-4062689069.</ref> Other schools, such as the {{ill|Koitijo school|ja|小一条流}} of Morosuke’s fifth son and the later {{ill|Mido school|ja|御堂流}}, also emerged.
During the {{ill|Cloistered rule|ja|院政|Cloistered rule=Cloistered rule|lt=insei}} period, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado school|ja|土御門流}} and the {{ill|Hanazono school|ja|花園流}} were formed, the latter completed by figures such as {{ill|Tokudaiji Sanetsune|ja|徳大寺実定}} and {{ill|Sanjō Sanefusa|ja|三条実房}}. In the late Heian period, {{ill|Matsudono Motofusa|ja|松殿基房}} synthesized many traditions into what became known as the "Matsudono Kanpaku Theory," which influenced court society for centuries.
As {{ill|kanji-uke|ja|官司請負制}} became widespread, some families such as the {{ill|Tokudaiji family|ja|徳大寺家}} (Kujo school) and the {{ill|Ōi no Mikado family|ja|大炊御門家}} (Mido school) adopted yūsoku kojitsu as their professional legacy.
=== Bushi Traditions ===
In the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} emphasized bushi traditions. Over time, practices from Kyoto and local customs blended to form the foundations of samurai etiquette. Schools like the {{ill|Ogasawara school|ja|小笠原流}} and {{ill|Ise school|ja|伊勢流}} emerged during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}.
== Edo Period ==
During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, independent researchers such as {{ill|Tsuboi Yoshitomo|ja|壺井義知}} gained recognition. Influential figures from the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|公家}} and {{ill|samurai class|ja|武家}} further elevated the academic level of these studies. Samurai experts like {{ill|Ise Sadatake|ja|伊勢貞丈}} contributed to many texts, while court nobles such as {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokikata|ja|平松時方}} and {{ill|Uramatsu Kōyo|ja|裏松光世}} advanced public and private yūsoku traditions.
== Modern Period ==
With the abolition of traditional court and samurai systems in the modern period, yūsoku kojitsu lost its practical significance. However, it continued to be studied as part of {{ill|historical studies|ja|歴史学}} or Japanese literary studies, with contributions from scholars like {{ill|Sato Makoto|ja|佐藤誠実}} and {{ill|Wada Hidematsu|ja|和田英松}}.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist|group="注釈"}} {{Reflist}}

[[ja:佐藤誠実]] [[ja:和田英松]] *
== See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en}}[[ja:有職]] * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en}}[[ja:朝儀]] * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en}}[[ja:宮中祭祀]] * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en}}[[ja:日本の官制]] * {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|fr|Zenken kojitsu|en}}[[ja:前賢故実]] * {{ill|Historical verification|en}}[[ja:時代考証]]
[[ja:有職故実]]

'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草}}, [[Edo]] (present-day {{ill|Taitō Ward|ja|台東区}}, [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|ja|元老院 (日本)}}, a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Kojiruien|ja|古事類苑}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): * {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} * {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}}

'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草}}, [[Edo]] (present-day {{ill|Taitō Ward|ja|台東区}}, [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|ja|元老院 (日本)}}, a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Kojiruien|ja|古事類苑}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): * {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} * {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} * {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}}
== See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[Kokugaku]] (国学) * {{ill|Kojiruien|ja|古事類苑}}

revision.text:
'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草}}, [[Edo]] (present-day {{ill|Taitō Ward|ja|台東区}}, [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|ja|元老院 (日本)}}, a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Kojiruien|ja|古事類苑}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌}}'' (1899–1900)

revision.text:
'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|qq=}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|qq=}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:幕末の学者
revision.text:
'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|qq=}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|qq=}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:文学博士取得者
revision.text:
'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|qq=}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899–1900)

revision.text:
'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|qq=}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899–1900)

== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}

revision.text:
'''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[Edo period]] to the [[Meiji period]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|qq=}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|qq=}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|qq=}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]

== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|qid=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].

His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.

== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|qq=|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|qq=|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|qq=|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|qq=|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|qq=|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|qq=|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|qq=|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * [[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]] * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * [[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]] * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|佐藤誠実|version=102138932|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * [[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]] * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * [[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]] * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|佐藤誠実|version=102138932|comment=Imported full ja history}}

=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
