revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * [[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]] * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[commons:" to "[[co:commons:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (national studies) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]], [[:en:Edo|Edo]] (present-day [[:en:Taitō|Taitō Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the [[:en:Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|Ministry of Education]] (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the [[:en:Genrōin|Genrōin]], a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] (国学) * [[:en:Koji Ruien|Kojiruien]]
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * [[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]] * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}, {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo}} (present-day {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|en|Genrōin|WD=Q1502583|lt=Genrōin}}, a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106364647]] Wikidata name: Satō Jōjitsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} (national studies) from the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}.
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}, {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo}} (present-day {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|WD=Q1054379|lt=Ministry of Education}} (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|en|Genrōin|WD=Q1502583|lt=Genrōin}}, a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} (国学) * {{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien}}
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu}} * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540}}}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} (national studies) from the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}.
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}, {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo}} (present-day {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|WD=Q1054379|lt=Ministry of Education}} (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|en|Genrōin|WD=Q1502583|lt=Genrōin}}, a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} (国学) * {{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien}}
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu}} * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540|1=Scholar}}}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} (national studies) from the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}.
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa|1=Asakusa}}, {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo|1=Edo}} (present-day {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō Ward|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|WD=Q1054379|lt=Ministry of Education|1=Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology}} (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|en|Genrōin|WD=Q1502583|lt=Genrōin|1=Genrōin}}, a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien|1=Koji Ruien}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} (国学) * {{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien|1=Koji Ruien}}
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}} * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} (国学) * {{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien|1=Koji Ruien}}
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}} * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Satō Jōjitsu]][[ja:佐藤誠実]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐藤誠実]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1839; {{birth date|1839|12|28}}|death_date=1908; {{death date|1908|3|31}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|scholar|WD=Q20826540|1=Scholar}}}} '''Satō Jōjitsu''' (佐藤 誠実, also read as Satō Nobuzane; December 28, 1839 – March 31, 1908) was a Japanese scholar of {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} (national studies) from the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}.
== Biography == Satō was born at {{ill|Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja|正行寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogyōji Temple (Taitō Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Jōdo Shinshū temple in {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa|1=Asakusa}}, {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo|1=Edo}} (present-day {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō Ward|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}). His childhood name was Zōshu, and his pseudonym was Mokuzai.
From a young age, he served as the head priest of his family temple. He later studied Chinese classics under {{ill|Azumi Gonsai|ja|安積艮斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Gonsai|1=Asaka Gonsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4071127}} and Kokugaku under {{ill|Kurokawa Harumura|ja|黒川春村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Harumura|1=Kurokawa Harumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536184}}.
After the [[Meiji Restoration]], in 1872, he joined the {{ill|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|WD=Q1054379|lt=Ministry of Education|1=Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology}} (then known as the Ministry of Education). Later, he worked at the {{ill|Genrōin|en|Genrōin|WD=Q1502583|lt=Genrōin|1=Genrōin}}, a precursor to the [[:en:Imperial Diet (Japan)|Imperial Diet]].
In 1880, he contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien|1=Koji Ruien}}'', a comprehensive encyclopedic work, alongside {{ill|Hiroike Chikurō|ja|廣池千九郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroike Chikurō|1=Chikurō Hiroike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486370}}. When the project was transferred to the [[Jingū Administration Office]] in 1895, he was appointed as the chief editor.
In recognition of his contributions, he was awarded the title of [[Doctor of Letters (Japan)|Doctor of Letters]] in 1899.
== Academic Career == In 1883, he became a lecturer in the classics department at [[:en:Tokyo University|Tokyo University]] (later {{ill|Tokyo Imperial University|ja|東京帝国大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京大学|1=University of Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7842}}), and in 1891, he was appointed a professor of classical studies at the {{ill|Tokyo Music School (former)|ja|東京音楽学校 (旧制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Music School (former)|1=Tokyo Academy of Music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577897}}.
== Major Works == === Books === * ''Nihon Kyōikushi'' (日本教育史, ''History of Japanese Education'') – A textbook for normal schools (師範学校): ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻上 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 1)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357}} ** {{Cite book|title=日本教育史 修訂 巻下 (Nihon Kyōikushi Shūtei, Vol. 2)|year=1943|publisher=十一組出版部|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1461357/1/156}} * ''Satō Seijitsu Hakushi Ritsuryō Kakushiki Ronshū'' (佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集) – Collection of his research on the Ritsuryō system (1991, {{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}) * ''Gogaku Shinan'' (語学指南, ''Guide to Language Studies'', 1879): ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻1|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992000}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻2|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992001}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻3|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992002}} ** {{Cite book|title=語学指南 巻4|year=1879|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/992003}}
=== Selected Papers === * ''訓点之誤'' (Errors in Kunten Annotations), in ''{{ill|Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja|大八州学会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyashimagakai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''五十音考'' (A Study of the Japanese Syllabary), in ''{{ill|Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja|東京茗渓会雑誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyō Meikeikai Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1891) * ''続日本紀を上る表の約解'' (Annotated Table of the Shoku Nihongi), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899) * ''律令考'' (A Study of the Ritsuryō System), serialized in ''{{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1899–1900)
== References == * {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Yamamoto Kidō|ja|山本毅堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Kidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1908|title=文学博士佐藤誠実先生小傳|journal={{ill|Kokugakuin Journal|ja|國學院雜誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin Journal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|volume=14|issue=4|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3364806/1/36}} * {{Cite book|title=佐藤誠実博士律令格式論集|editor={{ill|Takikawa Masajirō|ja|瀧川政次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikawa Masajirō|1=Masajirō Takikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566311}}|publisher={{ill|Kujaku Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujaku Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552315}}|year=1992|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/13097740}} == See Also == * [[Ritsuryō system]] (律令) * {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} (国学) * {{ill|Koji Ruien|en|Koji Ruien|WD=Q701081|lt=Kojiruien|1=Koji Ruien}}
* == See Also == * {{ill|Yūsoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūsoku|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial rituals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial rituals|qq=}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese governmental systems|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese governmental systems|qq=}} * {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}} * {{ill|Historical verification|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical verification|qq=}}
